summary: Selection bias—specifically admission bias/Berkson bias—is identified because it stems from restricting analysis to hospitalized patients who have higher probabilities of being exposed due to illness severity. Hospital-based sampling creates a biased sample not representative of the broader population, leading to overestimation of associations between factors and outcomes.
